response: The internet's development began in the 1960s with U.S. Department of Defense-funded research, leading to the creation of ARPANET, which sent its first message in 1969. Key milestones include the adoption of TCP/IP in 1983, the invention of the World Wide Web by Tim Berners-Lee in 1989, and the launch of the Mosaic web browser in 1993, which made the web accessible to the general public. The dot-com boom and subsequent rise of social media and mobile internet, along with the emergence of cloud computing, have transformed the internet into a ubiquitous platform connecting over 5 billion people and integral to all aspects of modern life.
